speaker: FLORIZELL
text: These your unusual weeds to each part of you

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Does give a life—no shepherdess, but Flora

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Peering in April’s front. This your sheep-shearing

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Is as a meeting of the petty gods,

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: And you the queen on ’t.

speaker: PERDITA
text: Sir, my gracious lord,

speaker: PERDITA
text: To chide at your extremes it not becomes me;

speaker: PERDITA
text: O, pardon that I name them! Your high self,

speaker: PERDITA
text: The gracious mark o’ th’ land, you have obscured

speaker: PERDITA
text: With a swain’s wearing, and me, poor lowly maid,

speaker: PERDITA
text: Most goddesslike pranked up. But that our feasts

speaker: PERDITA
text: In every mess have folly, and the feeders

speaker: PERDITA
text: Digest it with a custom, I should blush

speaker: PERDITA
text: To see you so attired, swoon, I think,

speaker: PERDITA
text: To show myself a glass.

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: I bless the time

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: When my good falcon made her flight across

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Thy father’s ground.

speaker: PERDITA
text: Now Jove afford you cause.

speaker: PERDITA
text: To me the difference forges dread. Your greatness

speaker: PERDITA
text: Hath not been used to fear. Even now I tremble

speaker: PERDITA
text: To think your father by some accident

speaker: PERDITA
text: Should pass this way as you did. O the Fates,

speaker: PERDITA
text: How would he look to see his work, so noble,

speaker: PERDITA
text: Vilely bound up? What would he say? Or how

speaker: PERDITA
text: Should I, in these my borrowed flaunts, behold

speaker: PERDITA
text: The sternness of his presence?

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Apprehend

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Nothing but jollity. The gods themselves,

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Humbling their deities to love, have taken

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: The shapes of beasts upon them. Jupiter

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Became a bull, and bellowed; the green Neptune

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: A ram, and bleated; and the fire-robed god,

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Golden Apollo, a poor humble swain,

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: As I seem now. Their transformations

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Were never for a piece of beauty rarer,

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Nor in a way so chaste, since my desires

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Run not before mine honor, nor my lusts

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Burn hotter than my faith.

speaker: PERDITA
text: O, but sir,

speaker: PERDITA
text: Your resolution cannot hold when ’tis

speaker: PERDITA
text: Opposed, as it must be, by th’ power of the King.

speaker: PERDITA
text: One of these two must be necessities,

speaker: PERDITA
text: Which then will speak: that you must change this purpose

speaker: PERDITA
text: Or I my life.

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Thou dear’st Perdita,

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: With these forced thoughts I prithee darken not

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: The mirth o’ th’ feast. Or I’ll be thine, my fair,

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Or not my father’s. For I cannot be

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Mine own, nor anything to any, if

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: I be not thine. To this I am most constant,

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Though destiny say no. Be merry, gentle.

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Strangle such thoughts as these with anything

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: That you behold the while. Your guests are coming.

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Lift up your countenance as it were the day

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Of celebration of that nuptial which

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: We two have sworn shall come.

speaker: PERDITA
text: O Lady Fortune,

speaker: PERDITA
text: Stand you auspicious!

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: See, your guests approach.

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Address yourself to entertain them sprightly,

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: And let’s be red with mirth.

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: Fie, daughter, when my old wife lived, upon

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: This day she was both pantler, butler, cook,

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: Both dame and servant; welcomed all; served all;

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: Would sing her song and dance her turn, now here

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: At upper end o’ th’ table, now i’ th’ middle;

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: On his shoulder, and his; her face afire

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: With labor, and the thing she took to quench it

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: She would to each one sip. You are retired

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: As if you were a feasted one and not

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: The hostess of the meeting. Pray you bid

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: These unknown friends to ’s welcome, for it is

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: A way to make us better friends, more known.

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: Come, quench your blushes and present yourself

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: That which you are, mistress o’ th’ feast. Come on,

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: And bid us welcome to your sheep-shearing,

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: As your good flock shall prosper.

speaker: PERDITA
text: Sir, welcome.

speaker: PERDITA
text: It is my father’s will I should take on me

speaker: PERDITA
text:
The hostess-ship o’ th’ day.
                 
                To
                 
                Camillo
                .
               You’re welcome, sir.—

speaker: PERDITA
text: Give me those flowers there, Dorcas.—Reverend sirs,

speaker: PERDITA
text: For you there’s rosemary and rue. These keep

speaker: PERDITA
text: Seeming and savor all the winter long.

speaker: PERDITA
text: Grace and remembrance be to you both,

speaker: PERDITA
text: And welcome to our shearing.

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Shepherdess—

speaker: POLIXENES
text: A fair one are you—well you fit our ages

speaker: POLIXENES
text: With flowers of winter.

speaker: PERDITA
text: Sir, the year growing ancient,

speaker: PERDITA
text: Not yet on summer’s death nor on the birth

speaker: PERDITA
text: Of trembling winter, the fairest flowers o’ th’ season

speaker: PERDITA
text: Are our carnations and streaked gillyvors,

speaker: PERDITA
text: Which some call nature’s bastards. Of that kind

speaker: PERDITA
text: Our rustic garden’s barren, and I care not

speaker: PERDITA
text: To get slips of them.

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Wherefore, gentle maiden,

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Do you neglect them?

speaker: PERDITA
text: For I have heard it said

speaker: PERDITA
text: There is an art which in their piedness shares

speaker: PERDITA
text: With great creating nature.

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Say there be;

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Yet nature is made better by no mean

speaker: POLIXENES
text: But nature makes that mean. So, over that art

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Which you say adds to nature is an art

speaker: POLIXENES
text: That nature makes. You see, sweet maid, we marry

speaker: POLIXENES
text: A gentler scion to the wildest stock,

speaker: POLIXENES
text: And make conceive a bark of baser kind

speaker: POLIXENES
text: By bud of nobler race. This is an art

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Which does mend nature, change it rather, but

speaker: POLIXENES
text: The art itself is nature.

speaker: PERDITA
text: So it is.

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Then make your garden rich in gillyvors,

speaker: POLIXENES
text: And do not call them bastards.

speaker: PERDITA
text: I’ll not put

speaker: PERDITA
text: The dibble in earth to set one slip of them,

speaker: PERDITA
text: No more than, were I painted, I would wish

speaker: PERDITA
text: This youth should say ’twere well, and only therefore

speaker: PERDITA
text: Desire to breed by me. Here’s flowers for you:

speaker: PERDITA
text: Hot lavender, mints, savory, marjoram,

speaker: PERDITA
text: The marigold, that goes to bed wi’ th’ sun

speaker: PERDITA
text: And with him rises weeping. These are flowers

speaker: PERDITA
text: Of middle summer, and I think they are given

speaker: PERDITA
text: To men of middle age. You’re very welcome.

speaker: CAMILLO
text: I should leave grazing, were I of your flock,

speaker: CAMILLO
text: And only live by gazing.

speaker: PERDITA
text: Out, alas!

speaker: PERDITA
text: You’d be so lean that blasts of January

speaker: PERDITA
text: Would blow you through and through.

speaker: PERDITA
text: Now, my fair’st friend,

speaker: PERDITA
text: I would I had some flowers o’ th’ spring, that might

speaker: PERDITA
text:
Become your time of day,
                 
                (
                to
                 
                the
                 
                Shepherdesses
                )
              and yours, and yours,

speaker: PERDITA
text: That wear upon your virgin branches yet

speaker: PERDITA
text: Your maidenheads growing. O Proserpina,

speaker: PERDITA
text: For the flowers now that, frighted, thou let’st fall

speaker: PERDITA
text: From Dis’s wagon! Daffodils,

speaker: PERDITA
text: That come before the swallow dares, and take

speaker: PERDITA
text: The winds of March with beauty; violets dim,

speaker: PERDITA
text: But sweeter than the lids of Juno’s eyes

speaker: PERDITA
text: Or Cytherea’s breath; pale primroses,

speaker: PERDITA
text: That die unmarried ere they can behold

speaker: PERDITA
text: Bright Phoebus in his strength—a malady

speaker: PERDITA
text: Most incident to maids; bold oxlips and

speaker: PERDITA
text: The crown imperial; lilies of all kinds,

speaker: PERDITA
text: The flower-de-luce being one—O, these I lack

speaker: PERDITA
text: To make you garlands of, and my sweet friend,

speaker: PERDITA
text: To strew him o’er and o’er.

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: What, like a corse?

speaker: PERDITA
text: No, like a bank for love to lie and play on,

speaker: PERDITA
text: Not like a corse; or if, not to be buried,

speaker: PERDITA
text: But quick and in mine arms. Come, take your flowers.

speaker: PERDITA
text: Methinks I play as I have seen them do

speaker: PERDITA
text: In Whitsun pastorals. Sure this robe of mine

speaker: PERDITA
text: Does change my disposition.

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: What you do

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Still betters what is done. When you speak, sweet,

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: I’d have you do it ever. When you sing,

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: I’d have you buy and sell so, so give alms,

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Pray so; and for the ord’ring your affairs,

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: To sing them too. When you do dance, I wish you

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: A wave o’ th’ sea, that you might ever do

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Nothing but that, move still, still so,

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: And own no other function. Each your doing,

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: So singular in each particular,

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Crowns what you are doing in the present deeds,

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: That all your acts are queens.

speaker: PERDITA
text: O Doricles,

speaker: PERDITA
text: Your praises are too large. But that your youth

speaker: PERDITA
text: And the true blood which peeps fairly through ’t

speaker: PERDITA
text: Do plainly give you out an unstained shepherd,

speaker: PERDITA
text: With wisdom I might fear, my Doricles,

speaker: PERDITA
text: You wooed me the false way.

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: I think you have

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: As little skill to fear as I have purpose

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: To put you to ’t. But come, our dance, I pray.

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Your hand, my Perdita. So turtles pair

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: That never mean to part.

speaker: PERDITA
text: I’ll swear for ’em.

speaker: POLIXENES
text: This is the prettiest lowborn lass that ever

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Ran on the greensward. Nothing she does or seems

speaker: POLIXENES
text: But smacks of something greater than herself,

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Too noble for this place.

speaker: CAMILLO
text: He tells her something

speaker: CAMILLO
text: That makes her blood look out. Good sooth, she is

speaker: CAMILLO
text: The queen of curds and cream.

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: Come on, strike up.

speaker: DORCAS
text: Mopsa must be your mistress? Marry, garlic

speaker: DORCAS
text: To mend her kissing with.

speaker: MOPSA
text: Now, in good time!

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: Not a word, a word. We stand upon our manners.—

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: Come, strike up.

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Pray, good shepherd, what fair swain is this

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Which dances with your daughter?

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: They call him Doricles, and boasts himself

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: To have a worthy feeding. But I have it

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: Upon his own report, and I believe it.

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: He looks like sooth. He says he loves my daughter.

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: I think so too, for never gazed the moon

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: Upon the water as he’ll stand and read,

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: As ’twere, my daughter’s eyes. And, to be plain,

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: I think there is not half a kiss to choose

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: Who loves another best.

speaker: POLIXENES
text: She dances featly.

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: So she does anything, though I report it

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: That should be silent. If young Doricles

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: Do light upon her, she shall bring him that

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: Which he not dreams of.

speaker: SERVANT
text: O, master, if you did but hear the peddler at

speaker: SERVANT
text: the door, you would never dance again after a tabor

speaker: SERVANT
text: and pipe; no, the bagpipe could not move you. He

speaker: SERVANT
text: sings several tunes faster than you’ll tell money. He

speaker: SERVANT
text: utters them as he had eaten ballads and all men’s

speaker: SERVANT
text: ears grew to his tunes.

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: He could never come better. He shall

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: come in. I love a ballad but even too well if it be

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: doleful matter merrily set down, or a very pleasant

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: thing indeed and sung lamentably.

speaker: SERVANT
text: He hath songs for man or woman, of all sizes.

speaker: SERVANT
text: No milliner can so fit his customers with gloves. He

speaker: SERVANT
text: has the prettiest love songs for maids, so without

speaker: SERVANT
text: bawdry, which is strange, with such delicate burdens

speaker: SERVANT
text: of dildos and fadings, “Jump her and thump

speaker: SERVANT
text: her.” And where some stretch-mouthed rascal

speaker: SERVANT
text: would, as it were, mean mischief and break a foul

speaker: SERVANT
text: gap into the matter, he makes the maid to answer

speaker: SERVANT
text: “Whoop, do me no harm, good man”; puts him off,

speaker: SERVANT
text: slights him, with “Whoop, do me no harm, good

speaker: SERVANT
text: man.”

speaker: POLIXENES
text: This is a brave fellow.

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: Believe me, thou talkest of an admirable

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: conceited fellow. Has he any unbraided

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: wares?

speaker: SERVANT
text: He hath ribbons of all the colors i’ th’ rainbow;

speaker: SERVANT
text: points more than all the lawyers in Bohemia

speaker: SERVANT
text: can learnedly handle, though they come to him by

speaker: SERVANT
text: th’ gross; inkles, caddises, cambrics, lawns—why,

speaker: SERVANT
text: he sings ’em over as they were gods or goddesses.

speaker: SERVANT
text: You would think a smock were a she-angel, he so

speaker: SERVANT
text: chants to the sleeve-hand and the work about the

speaker: SERVANT
text: square on ’t.

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: Prithee bring him in, and let him

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: approach singing.

speaker: PERDITA
text: Forewarn him that he use no scurrilous words

speaker: PERDITA
text: in ’s tunes.

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: You have of these peddlers that have

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: more in them than you’d think, sister.

speaker: PERDITA
text: Ay, good brother, or go about to think.

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: Lawn as white as driven snow,

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: Cypress black as e’er was crow,

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: Gloves as sweet as damask roses,

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: Masks for faces and for noses,

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: Bugle bracelet, necklace amber,

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: Perfume for a lady’s chamber,

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: Golden coifs and stomachers

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: For my lads to give their dears,

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: Pins and poking-sticks of steel,

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: What maids lack from head to heel,

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: Come buy of me, come. Come buy, come buy.

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: Buy, lads, or else your lasses cry.

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: Come buy.

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: If I were not in love with Mopsa, thou

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: shouldst take no money of me; but being enthralled

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: as I am, it will also be the bondage of certain

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: ribbons and gloves.

speaker: MOPSA
text: I was promised them against the feast, but they

speaker: MOPSA
text: come not too late now.

speaker: DORCAS
text: He hath promised you more than that, or there

speaker: DORCAS
text: be liars.

speaker: MOPSA
text: He hath paid you all he promised you. Maybe

speaker: MOPSA
text: he has paid you more, which will shame you to give

speaker: MOPSA
text: him again.

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: Is there no manners left among

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: maids? Will they wear their plackets where they

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: should bear their faces? Is there not milking time,

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: when you are going to bed, or kiln-hole, to whistle

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: of these secrets, but you must be tittle-tattling

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: before all our guests? ’Tis well they are whisp’ring.

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: Clamor your tongues, and not a word more.

speaker: MOPSA
text: I have done. Come, you promised me a tawdry

speaker: MOPSA
text: lace and a pair of sweet gloves.

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: Have I not told thee how I was cozened

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: by the way and lost all my money?

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: And indeed, sir, there are cozeners abroad;

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: therefore it behooves men to be wary.

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: Fear not thou, man. Thou shalt lose

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: nothing here.

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: I hope so, sir, for I have about me many

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: parcels of charge.

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: What hast here? Ballads?

speaker: MOPSA
text: Pray now, buy some. I love a ballad in print

speaker: MOPSA
text: alife, for then we are sure they are true.

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: Here’s one to a very doleful tune, how a

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: usurer’s wife was brought to bed of twenty moneybags

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: at a burden, and how she longed to eat adders’

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: heads and toads carbonadoed.

speaker: MOPSA
text: Is it true, think you?

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: Very true, and but a month old.

speaker: DORCAS
text: Bless me from marrying a usurer!

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: Here’s the midwife’s name to ’t, one Mistress

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: Taleporter, and five or six honest wives that

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: were present. Why should I carry lies abroad?

speaker: MOPSA
text: Pray you now, buy it.

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: Come on, lay it by, and

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: let’s first see more ballads. We’ll buy the other

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: things anon.

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: Here’s another ballad, of a fish that appeared

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: upon the coast on Wednesday the fourscore

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: of April, forty thousand fathom above water, and

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: sung this ballad against the hard hearts of maids. It

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: was thought she was a woman, and was turned into

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: a cold fish for she would not exchange flesh with

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: one that loved her. The ballad is very pitiful, and as

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: true.

speaker: DORCAS
text: Is it true too, think you?

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: Five justices’ hands at it, and witnesses

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: more than my pack will hold.

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: Lay it by too. Another.

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: This is a merry ballad, but a very pretty

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: one.

speaker: MOPSA
text: Let’s have some merry ones.

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: Why, this is a passing merry one and goes

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: to the tune of Two Maids Wooing a Man. There’s

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: scarce a maid westward but she sings it. ’Tis in

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: request, I can tell you.

speaker: MOPSA
text: We can both sing it. If thou ’lt bear a part, thou

speaker: MOPSA
text: shalt hear; ’tis in three parts.

speaker: DORCAS
text: We had the tune on ’t a month ago.

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: I can bear my part. You must know ’tis my

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: occupation. Have at it with you.

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: Get you hence, for I must go

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: Where it fits not you to know.

speaker: DORCAS
text: Whither?

speaker: MOPSA
text: O, whither?

speaker: DORCAS
text: Whither?

speaker: MOPSA
text: It becomes thy oath full well

speaker: MOPSA
text: Thou to me thy secrets tell.

speaker: DORCAS
text: Me too. Let me go thither.

speaker: MOPSA
text: Or thou goest to th’ grange or mill.

speaker: DORCAS
text: If to either, thou dost ill.

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: Neither.

speaker: DORCAS
text: What, neither?

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: Neither.

speaker: DORCAS
text: Thou hast sworn my love to be.

speaker: MOPSA
text: Thou hast sworn it more to me.

speaker: MOPSA
text: Then whither goest? Say whither.

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: had not left a purse alive in the whole army.

text: Nay, but my letters, by this means being there

text: So soon as you arrive, shall clear that doubt.

text: I am a poor fellow, sir.

text: Why, be so still. Here’s nobody will steal that

text: must make an exchange. Therefore discase thee

I am a poor fellow, sir.

speaker: CAMILLO

speaker: FLORIZELL

speaker: FLORIZELL

speaker: AUTOLYCUS

speaker: CAMILLO

speaker: CAMILLO

text: I have a woman’s longing.

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Fortune speed us!—

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Thus we set on, Camillo, to th’ seaside.

speaker: CAMILLO
text: The swifter speed the better.

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: I understand the business; I hear it. To have

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: an open ear, a quick eye, and a nimble hand is

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: necessary for a cutpurse; a good nose is requisite

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: also, to smell out work for th’ other senses. I see this

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: is the time that the unjust man doth thrive. What an

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: exchange had this been without boot! What a boot

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: is here with this exchange! Sure the gods do this

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: year connive at us, and we may do anything extempore.

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: The Prince himself is about a piece of iniquity,

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: stealing away from his father with his clog at his

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: heels. If I thought it were a piece of honesty to

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: acquaint the King withal, I would not do ’t. I hold it

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: the more knavery to conceal it, and therein am I

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: constant to my profession.

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: Aside, aside! Here is more matter for a hot brain.

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: Every lane’s end, every shop, church, session, hanging,

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: yields a careful man work.

text: Very wisely, puppies.

text: Well, let us to the King. There is that in this

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: fardel will make him scratch his beard.

text: I know not what impediment this

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: complaint may be to the flight of my master.

              SON

text: Though I am not naturally honest,

text: I am so sometimes by chance. Let me pocket up my

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: peddler’s excrement.

text: aboard a new ship to purge melancholy and air

text: monster.

              SON

text: Not he alone shall suffer what wit can

text: make heavy and vengeance bitter; but those that are

text: germane to him, though removed fifty times, shall

text: into a sheepcote? All deaths are too few, the sharpest

text: too easy.

              SON

text: Well, give me the moiety.

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: Are you a party in this business?

              SON

              SON
text: be a pitiful one, I hope I shall not be flayed out of it.

text: O, that’s the case of the shepherd’s son!

text: Hang him, he’ll be made an example.

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: Comfort, good comfort.

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: We must to the King, and show our strange

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: sights. He must know ’tis none of your daughter nor

speaker:

              SON
text: my sister. We are gone else.—Sir, I will give you as

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: be brought you.

text: I will trust you. Walk before toward the

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: seaside. Go on the right hand. I will but look upon

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: the hedge, and follow you.

SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: We are blessed in this

speaker:
SHEPHERD’S
               
              SON
text: man, as I may say, even blessed.

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: Let’s before, as he bids us. He was provided

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: to do us good.

text: If I had a mind to be honest, I see Fortune

text: would not suffer me. She drops booties in my

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: mouth. I am courted now with a double occasion:

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: gold, and a means to do the Prince my master good;

speaker: AUTOLYCUS

speaker: AUTOLYCUS

speaker: AUTOLYCUS
text: blind ones, aboard him. If he think it fit to shore
